nodes.name: When Executed by WF-001
nodes.notes: Input data mode = Accept all data (passThrough). n8n source: when inputSource==PASSTHROUGH the trigger returns inputData unchanged, INCLUDING binary. Required so binary from the parent Execute Workflow node reaches this sub-workflow. typeVersion 1.1 (1 is deprecated).

nodes.name: Load OCR Provider Settings

parameters.query: SELECT settings FROM configuration.providers WHERE kind='ocr';

nodes.notes: Runtime endpoint/model/timeout (R-08). Bind CRIE Postgres credential.

nodes.name: Re-attach Binary (pattern)
nodes.notes: PATTERN — restore binary after a Postgres node that precedes a binary consumer. Any future SW workflow with Postgres-before-binary-consumer must apply this same re-attach-from-trigger step. See CRIE Workflow Engineering Guidelines.

nodes.name: submit() — POST Analyze

parameters.url: ={{ $('Load OCR Provider Settings').first().json.settings.endpoint }}/documentintelligence/documentModels/{{ $('Load OCR Provider Settings').first().json.settings.model }}:analyze?api-version=2024-11-30

nodes.notes: SUBMIT adapter (Azure). POST :analyze with the binary. fullResponse=true so the Operation-Location header is available. neverError=true so 202 is not treated as an error. Node-level retry handles transient 429/503 only — NOT operation progress.

nodes.name: submit() — capture pollUrl
nodes.notes: Captures the poll URL (Azure: Operation-Location) using provider config (settings.poll.pollUrlHeader), honors Retry-After for the first wait, and seeds the poll budget (maxAttempts / maxElapsedMs). A non-202 or missing header is a terminal submit failure.

nodes.name: poll() — wait
nodes.notes: Waits the interval computed by the poll adapter (honors Retry-After; default ~2s per Azure guidance).

nodes.name: poll() — GET operation
nodes.notes: GET the operation URL. fullResponse for Retry-After header. Node retry = transient faults only.

nodes.name: poll() — evaluate
nodes.notes: Normalises the provider poll response to {status,result} via config (statusPath, terminalSuccess/Failure, resultPath). Honors Retry-After, backs off on 429, increments the attempt counter, and enforces maxAttempts/maxElapsedMs so the loop can never run forever.

nodes.name: terminal?
nodes.notes: true -> terminal (succeeded/failed/timeout). false -> loop back to Wait and poll again.

nodes.name: succeeded?
nodes.notes: succeeded -> shapeResult(). failed/timeout -> ocr=null so WF-001's §154 OCR-fail path fires.

nodes.name: PROBE — raw Azure response structure
nodes.notes: Selects the terminal poll iteration by walking EVERY run index of 'poll() — evaluate' ($('X').all() returns only ONE run — runIndex defaults to 0 — which was returning a stale 'running' iteration with _result=null). Throws if no terminal run exists rather than falling back to stale data.

nodes.name: shapeResult() — OCR failed
nodes.notes: Emits ocr=null + ocrError. Feeds WF-001's existing §154 OCR failure branch unchanged.

meta.note: Provider call via HTTP Request node (portable). Credentials bound at import: azureApi + CRIE Postgres.
meta.enrichment: output = {...incomingItem, newResult}; never discards accumulated state
meta.jsonbFix: settings is jsonb (object); removed JSON.parse on settings; LLM-content parse retained
meta.triggerFix: executeWorkflowTrigger upgraded to 1.1 inputSource=passThrough for binary passthrough
meta.binaryReattach: Pattern node added: Postgres-before-binary-consumer -> restore binary from trigger by node reference
meta.lro: Azure LRO in-workflow poll loop: submit() -> poll() (Wait+GET+evaluate, Retry-After honored, 429 backoff, attempt/elapsed budget) -> shapeResult(). Output contract unchanged.
changelog: v2.1.0: shapeResult now carries Azure's real paragraph structure into ocr.pages[].paragraphs (with ocr.paragraphSource flag). Additive — existing contract fields unchanged.

v2.2.0: FIX — shapeResult/fail-out read the TERMINAL poll iteration via $('poll() — evaluate').all() instead of $json. In the Wait-node loop, $json could resolve to a stale iteration whose _result was null, producing ocr.pages=[] and ocr.raw={} downstream. Also throws if analyzeResult has no pages, instead of silently emitting an empty OCR object.

v2.3.0: ROOT-CAUSE FIX. $('poll() — evaluate').all() returns items of ONE run only (runIndex defaults to 0), so the previous terminalPoll() saw a single stale 'running' iteration with _result=null and fell back to it -> 'analyzeResult carried no pages (status=running)'. Now walks EVERY run index (0..runIndex) per the n8n canonical loop pattern, selects the terminal run, and throws rather than falling back to stale data. poll-eval logs each iteration.
